questions.question:
Suppose the time to service a page fault is on the average 10 milliseconds, while a memory access takes 1 microsecond. Then a 99.99% hit ratio results in average memory access time of

(GATE CSE 2000)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which of the following is/are advantage(s) of virtual memory?

(GATE CSE 1999)
options:
A.

Faster access to memory on an average.

B.

Processes can be given protected address spaces.

C.

Linker can assign addresses independent of where the program will be loaded in physical memory.

D.

Program larger than the physical memory size can be run.
questions.correct_answer:
B.

Processes can be given protected address spaces.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
A multi-user, multi-processing operating system cannot be implemented on hardware that does not support

(GATE CSE 1999)
options:
C.

At least two modes of CPU execution (privileged and non-privileged)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Listed below are some operating system abstractions (in the left column) and the hardware components (in the right column)
\(\small \begin{array}{cl|cl}\hline \text{(A)}& \text{Thread} & \text{1.}& \text{Interrupt} \\\hline \text{(B)}& \text{Virtual address space} & \text{2.}& \text{Memory} \\\hline \text{(C)} &\text{File system} & \text{3.} &\text{CPU} \\\hline \text{(D)} &\text{Signal} & \text{4.}& \text{Disk} \\\hline \end{array}\)

(GATE CSE 1999)

questions.question:
If an instruction takes \(i\) microseconds and a page fault takes an additional \(j\) microseconds, the effective instruction time if on the average a page fault occurs every \(k\) instruction is:

(GATE CSE 1998)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Dirty bit for a page in a page table

(GATE CSE 1997)
options:
A.

helps avoid unnecessary writes on a paging device
questions.correct_answer:
A.

helps avoid unnecessary writes on a paging device
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Thrashing

(GATE CSE 1997)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Locality of reference implies that the page reference being made by a process

(GATE CSE 1997)
options:
A.

will always be to the page used in the previous page reference

B.

is likely to be to one of the pages used in the last few page references

C.

will always be to one of the pages existing in memory

D.

will always lead to a page fault
questions.correct_answer:
B.

is likely to be to one of the pages used in the last few page references
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

(GATE CSE 1996)

questions.question:
In a virtual memory system the address space specified by the address lines of the CPU must be _____ than the physical memory size and ____ than the secondary storage size.

(GATE CSE 1995)

questions.question:
The address sequence generated by tracing a particular program executing in a pure demand based paging system with 100 records per page with 1 free main memory frame is recorded as follows. What is the number of page faults?
0100, 0200, 0430, 0499, 0510, 0530, 0560, 0120, 0220, 0240, 0260, 0320, 0370

(GATE CSE 1995)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which of the following page replacement algorithms suffers from Belady's anamoly?

(GATE CSE 1995)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
In a paged segmented scheme of memory management, the segment table itself must have a page table because

(GATE CSE 1995)
options:
A.

The segment table is often too large to fit in one page

B.

Each segment is spread over a number of pages

C.

Segment tables point to page tables and not to the physical locations of the segment

D.

The processor's description base register points to a page table
questions.correct_answer:
A.

The segment table is often too large to fit in one page
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following heap (figure) in which blank regions are not in use and hatched region are in use.

The sequence of requests for blocks of sizes 300, 25, 125, 50 can be satisfied if we use

(GATE CSE 1994)
options:
A.

either first fit or best fit policy (any one)

B.

first fit but not best fit policy

C.

best fit but not first fit policy
questions.correct_answer:
B.

first fit but not best fit policy
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which one of the following statements is true?

(GATE CSE 1994)
options:
A.

Macro definitions cannot appear within other macro definitions in assembly language programs

B.

Overlaying is used to run a program which is longer than the address space of a computer

C.

Virtual memory can be used to accommodate a program which is longer than the address space of a computer

D.

It is not possible to write interrupt service routines in a high level language
questions.correct_answer:
A.

Macro definitions cannot appear within other macro definitions in assembly language programs
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
